Assemble multiple RTF files into one

assemble_rtf.Rd

Concatenates two or more RTF files generated by generate_rtfreport into a single RTF document. The document header (font table, colour table, page settings) is taken from the first input file; subsequent files contribute only their section content, separated by \sect.

This is the standard way to produce a full TFL package (T14.1.1, T14.2.1, …) as one deliverable RTF.

Usage

assemble_rtf(input_files, output_file, overwrite = FALSE)

Arguments

input_files

Character vector of paths to RTF files to combine. Must contain at least 2 elements. Files are included in order.

output_file

Path for the assembled output RTF file.

overwrite

Logical; whether to overwrite an existing output file. Default FALSE.

Value

Invisibly returns output_file.

Page numbering

Page-number fields work correctly across the assembled document when the header/footer text uses dynamic tokens:

  • {AUTO_PAGE} — per-page number rendered by the RTF viewer.

  • {AUTO_TOTAL_PAGES} — total-page NUMPAGES field rendered by the RTF viewer across all assembled files.

The static token {TOTAL_PAGES} reflects only the page count of each source file and will show the wrong total in the assembled output.

See also

generate_rtfreport, rtf_document

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
  library(magrittr)

  # Build two reports and assemble them.
  make_report <- function(df, hdr) {
    rtf_document() %>%
      rtf_section(page = 1, secinfo = list(header = rtf_header(c(l = hdr)))) %>%
      rtf_tables(list(df))
  }

  f1 <- tempfile(fileext = ".rtf")
  f2 <- tempfile(fileext = ".rtf")
  generate_rtfreport(make_report(mtcars[1:3, ], "Table 1"), f1, overwrite = TRUE)
  generate_rtfreport(make_report(mtcars[4:6, ], "Table 2"), f2, overwrite = TRUE)

  out <- tempfile(fileext = ".rtf")
  assemble_rtf(c(f1, f2), out, overwrite = TRUE)
} # }
